detailed_explanation: Unfortunately; expresses regret that something is not as hoped. Polite refusal starter.
example_sentences: 生憎、その日は都合が悪いです - Unfortunately, I'm not available that day
生憎ながら、在庫がありません - Regrettably, we're out of stock
あいにく雨が降ってきた - Unfortunately, it started raining
usage_notes: 生憎ながら = formal/polite apology. Used for declining invitations. Cannot be used for fortunate situations.

detailed_explanation: Clearly obvious, evident, not hidden. Used when something is apparent to anyone.
example_sentences: 明らかに間違っている - Clearly wrong
彼の方が明らかに正しい - He's obviously right
明らかな嘘 - Obvious lie
usage_notes: 明らかだ (na-adj). 明らかに (adverb). Used for visible/obvious facts.

detailed_explanation: To give up, abandon hope, resign oneself. Acceptance of defeat/impossibility.
example_sentences: 諦めないで - Don't give up
もう諦めた - I've given up already
諦めるのは早い - It's too early to give up
usage_notes: 諦める = transitive (諦めさせる = make someone give up). Common in persistence contexts.

detailed_explanation: To open naturally, to be open. Intransitive - happens without agent.
example_sentences: ドアが開く - The door opens
店が開く時間 - Time store opens
口が開いた - Mouth opened (surprise)
usage_notes: 開く (hiraku) = physical opening. 空く (aku) = become empty. Contrast: 開ける (transitive).

detailed_explanation: To raise, lift, elevate something. Transitive counterpart to 上がる.
example_sentences: 手を上げる - Raise hand
値段を上げる - Raise price
子供を上げる - Bring up child
usage_notes: Transitive (you raise something). Contrast: 上がる (intransitive rises). 年を上げる = grow older.

detailed_explanation: To become impatient, fret, lose composure from hurry or anxiety.
example_sentences: 焦らないでください - Please don't panic
焦ってミスした - Made mistake panicking
あまり焦るな - Don't be so impatient
usage_notes: 焦る = emotional state. 焦らせる = make someone impatient. Contrast with 急ぐ (physical hurry).

detailed_explanation: To give, bestow, confer. More formal/literary than あげる. Authority giving.
example_sentences: 賞を与える - Award prize
影響を与える - Give influence
与えられた時間 - Allotted time
usage_notes: 与える = formal giving (authority→subordinate). 与える permission/influence/punishment.

detailed_explanation: To become warm, warm oneself. Intransitive - natural warming or self-warming.
example_sentences: 部屋が暖まる - Room warms up
火で暖まる - Warm by fire
暖まってください - Please warm yourself
usage_notes: 暖まる = intransitive. 暖める = transitive (warming something). Used for weather/rooms/body.

detailed_explanation: To warm something, heat up. Transitive - agent warming something.
example_sentences: 部屋を暖める - Warm the room
手を暖める - Warm hands
ご飯を暖める - Warm up food
usage_notes: Transitive. Contrast: 暖まる (intransitive). 温める also used (slightly more internal warmth).

detailed_explanation: To handle, treat, deal with, manipulate. Physical or metaphorical handling.
example_sentences: 機械を扱う - Handle machinery
問題を扱う - Deal with problem
丁寧に扱う - Handle carefully
usage_notes: 扱う = physical handling OR topic treatment. 取り扱う = handle/treat (customer service).

detailed_explanation: People/things coming together naturally. Intransitive - no direct object.
example_sentences: みんなが集まる - Everyone gathers
集まってください - Please gather
場所に集まる - Gather at location
usage_notes: Intransitive. 集める = transitive (collecting deliberately). N3 re-review from N4.

detailed_explanation: Actively collecting or gathering. Transitive - takes direct object.
example_sentences: お金を集める - Collect money
みんなを集める - Gather everyone
情報を集める - Gather information
usage_notes: Transitive. Contrast: 集まる (intransitive). N3 re-review from N4.

detailed_explanation: To hit target, apply something to, make a guess. Multiple uses.
example_sentences: ボールを当てる - Hit ball (at target)
薬を当てる - Apply medicine
当ててみて - Try to guess
usage_notes: 当てる = hit/apply/guess. 当たる = hit correctly (intransitive). 当たる = be right/guess correctly.

detailed_explanation: To hit target (intransitive), be correct, strike (weather/light).
example_sentences: 当たった - Hit!/Correct!
予想が当たる - Prediction is right
太陽が当たる - Sun shines on
usage_notes: Intransitive. 当たる = correct guess, winning lottery. 当たる adversary = hit by enemy.

detailed_explanation: Hot weather/temperature. For objects/food use 熱い (same reading, different kanji).
example_sentences: 暑い日 - Hot day
今日は暑い - Today is hot
暑くなった - Became hot
usage_notes: 暑い = weather/ambient hot. 熱い = hot to touch (objects). Distinguish by kanji/context.

detailed_explanation: Hot to touch, feverish, passionate. For weather use 暑い (different kanji).
example_sentences: 熱いお茶 - Hot tea
額が熱い - Forehead is hot (fever)
熱いキス - Passionate kiss
usage_notes: 熱い = hot objects/body/fever. 暑い = weather. Same reading, different kanji = different meaning.

detailed_explanation: To overflow, spill over, brim with. Water or emotions.
example_sentences: 淖が溢れる - Water overflows
笑いが溢れる - Laughter bubbles over
溢れるほどの愛 - Overflowing love
usage_notes: 溢れる = literal overflow OR emotional abundance. 溢れる tears/laughter/love.

detailed_explanation: Sweet taste OR lenient/permissive. Also naive/indulgent.
example_sentences: 甘いケーキ - Sweet cake
甘い言葉 - Sweet words (flattery)
甘い考え - Naive thinking
甘やかす - Spoil (someone)
usage_notes: 甘い = sweet taste OR sweet words. 甘い考え = naive/optimistic. 甘やかす = spoil someone.

detailed_explanation: To be left over, remain, exceed. More than enough.
example_sentences: お金が余る - Money left over
余った時間 - Spare time
食べきれず余った - Left uneaten
usage_notes: 余る = remain/excess. 余り = remainder (noun). 余りに = too much (adv).

detailed_explanation: To express apology, regret. Important in Japanese social harmony.
example_sentences: 謝らなければ - Must apologize
誤ります - I apologize
謝らなかった - Didn't apologize
usage_notes: 謝る = N3 re-review. 謝み = apology. 謝る vs 謝罪する (formal). 誤る often written in hiragana.

detailed_explanation: To wash things, body parts, clothes. Common daily activity.
example_sentences: 手を洗う - Wash hands
車を洗う - Wash car
洗い物 - Washing (dishes)
usage_notes: N3 re-review from N4. 洗濯する for laundry specifically. 手を洗う before meals - important Japanese custom.
